steps.title: Find the sleep contrast
steps.content: The speaker goes to bed seeking rest, but then a mental journey begins. His thoughts turn toward the friend, and those thoughts keep his drooping eyelids open, so the main idea is about wakeful thinking rather than sleep.

steps.title: Separate literal from mental
steps.content: A says the speaker is asleep and dreaming, but the text says his eyes remain open. B treats the journey as a literal trip, though it happens in his head. C introduces a discussion with the friend, which the text never describes. The remaining choice captures the speaker's wakeful focus on the friend. The answer is D.
